summary: Full backend test pass on Travel Itinerary Builder. 13/13 pytest cases green covering health, auth (me/logout/non-admin 403), admin whitelist CRUD, providers CRUD + sort, experiences CRUD + bad provider 400 + cascade rename + filters + facets, itineraries CRUD, Excel export (sheet name, main traveler, Day 1 row, service row, Final price = subtotal*(1+markup/100)=240.0), stats, and bulk import (synthetic + real Roman Road Tours_2024.xlsx). All responses use custom *_id fields; no MongoDB _id leaked.

minor.issue: set_cookie uses samesite='none' + secure=True; will only work over HTTPS. Fine in prod, but cookie won't be set on plain HTTP local dev. Non-blocking; tests use Bearer token.
No de-duplication: re-uploading the same sheet creates duplicate experiences for the same provider. Consider an upsert key (provider_id+title) or a 'replace' flag.

Refuses delete if experiences exist (good), but DELETE /api/experiences/{id} of an exp linked via itinerary services doesn't validate references. Itinerary services hold experience_id snapshot so this is acceptable for the snapshot model, just noting it.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 896 lines; consider splitting into routers/{auth,admin,providers,experiences,itineraries,export,import}.py before it grows further.
Bootstrap-admin path in /api/auth/session is correct but silently grants admin to *any* first login: ensure deployment seeds an explicit admin allowed_email entry pre-launch.
import_provider_sheet has no row-count cap; a malicious large xlsx would block the event loop while parsing. Consider a max_rows guard and offloading openpyxl parse to a thread (run_in_threadpool).
Excel export totals are stored as numeric cells but with no number_format - downstream Sofi import should still parse, but applying '#,##0.00' would be more readable.
current_user reads expires_at and handles str/naive datetimes; nice. But session_token is queried unindexed - add a Mongo index on user_sessions.session_token (unique) for perf at scale.
@app.on_event('shutdown') is deprecated in modern FastAPI; migrate to lifespan handler.
CORS allow_origin_regex='.*' combined with allow_credentials=True is permissive; tighten to the frontend origin before production.
test_credentials: Bearer test_admin_session_token (admin) - already seeded. A tmp agent user (test.agent@example.com, token test_agent_session_token) was seeded during the run for the 403 test and removed in teardown.
seed_data_creation: Re-seeded the admin user/session (idempotent) at session start. Imported Roman Road Tours_2024.xlsx into MongoDB: 1 provider + 13 experiences (Italia/Rome/actividad) left in place intentionally as Italian demo data for the frontend, per instructions. All TEST_ prefixed providers/experiences/itineraries were cleaned up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully green. Frontend was NOT tested per the review_request (backend-only). When frontend tests come up, use Bearer 'test_admin_session_token' via Authorization header for API calls; remember the cookie set by /api/auth/session is samesite=none + secure so it only works over HTTPS. The DB already has 1 real provider (Roman Road Tours) + ~13 experiences (Italia/Rome) for visual testing. Re-seed admin via the mongosh command in /app/memory/test_credentials.md if the token ever stops working.
